title: placus bifrons
intro: Placus bifrons, also known as the two-lobed or two-headed plack, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Placus bifrons include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of various respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Placus bifrons include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made from the plant's leaves, flowers, and roots.
